Context:

[SOURCE: https://bibleask.org/who-is-the-creator-of-the-universe/#God_The_Creator_Of_All_Things]
CONTENT: Table of Contents
The [Bible](https://bibleask.org/what-is-the-bible/) opens with the foundational [truth](https://bibleask.org/what-is-truth/) that [God](https://bibleask.org/who-is-god/) is the Creator of [heaven](https://bibleask.org/is-heaven-a-real-place/) and [earth](https://bibleask.org/what-is-earth-to-god/). This declaration forms the basis for understanding [God](https://bibleask.org/who-is-god/)’s character, His authority, and His relationship with humanity. Scripture presents creation not as a random event but as an intentional act of a loving and powerful Creator who brought all things into existence for His purpose and glory. From Genesis to Revelation, the Word of [God](https://bibleask.org/who-is-god/) affirms that everything in the universe originates from Him and is sustained by His will.
Recognizing God as Creator shapes how we view ourselves, the world around us, and God’s plan of redemption. The same God who formed the world from nothing is the One who restores fallen humanity through [Jesus](https://bibleask.org/who-is-jesus/) [Christ](https://bibleask.org/what-is-the-meaning-of-christ/).
God The Creator Of All Things
The prophets, apostles, and [Jesus](https://bibleask.org/who-is-jesus/) [Christ](https://bibleask.org/what-is-the-meaning-of-christ/) Himself affirmed that God created all that exists. Isaiah declared, “Have you not known? Have you not heard? The everlasting God, the Lord, the Creator of the ends of the [earth](https://bibleask.org/what-is-earth-to-god/), neither faints nor is weary” [(Isaiah 40:28)](https://bibleask.org/bible/isaiah/40/28/). Creation reveals His infinite power, [wisdom](https://bibleask.org/what-does-the-bible-say-about-wisdom/), and majesty.
[Paul](https://bibleask.org/who-was-the-apostle-paul/) wrote of [Christ](https://bibleask.org/what-is-the-meaning-of-christ/)’s role in creation, saying, “For by Him all things were created that are in [heaven](https://bibleask.org/is-heaven-a-real-place/) and that are on [earth](https://bibleask.org/what-is-earth-to-god/), visible and invisible… All things were created through Him and for Him” [(Colossians 1:16)](https://bibleask.org/bible/colossians/1/16/). This means creation was a cooperative work of the Father, Son, and [Holy Spirit](https://bibleask.org/how-to-be-filled-with-holy-spirit/).
The Old Testament also affirms that everything originates from God. Jeremiah wrote, “He is the Maker of all things” [(Jeremiah 10:16)](https://bibleask.org/bible/jeremiah/1

[SOURCE: https://www.gotquestions.org/Creator-God.html]
CONTENT: Answer
One of the foundational truths of the Bible is that God is the Creator of all that is. One of the many passages to proclaim God as Creator is Isaiah 40:28, “Do you not know? Have you not heard? The LORD is the everlasting God, the Creator of the ends of the earth. He will not grow tired or weary, and his understanding no one can fathom.” All three Persons of the Trinity were involved in the creation of the universe (Genesis 1:2; Acts 17:24; Hebrews 1:2).
A creator is one who makes something new. People can “create” art projects, musical compositions, and physical structures; however, they always have something to work with. They begin with preexisting matter and form it in new ways. Even music and other intellectual creations have rhythms, rhymes, notes, instruments, and artistic mediums that provide structure and offer possibilities. What is commonly called “creativity” is more akin to synthesis. God had no such raw materials to work with. When we say that God is the Creator, we mean that He is truly creative, in a category all by Himself, because He started with
[nothing](https://www.gotquestions.org/creation-ex-nihilo.html)(Colossians 1:16).
Genesis 1:1 says that “in the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth.” That means that, before Genesis 1:1, there existed no heavens and no earth. God spoke them into being (Genesis 1:3, 6, 9). “In six days the Lord made the heavens and the earth, the sea, and all that is in them” (Exodus 20:11; cf. Revelation 10:6). He is God. He is not limited by our understanding, nor by time, space, or matter. As the Creator, God is the
[all-wise](https://www.gotquestions.org/God-omniscient.html)originator and designer of all things: “How many are your works, Lord! In wisdom you made them all” (Psalm 104:24).
When God created the heavens and the earth, He did so by speaking it into existence. When He created the things that filled the earth and the sky, He spoke them into being (Genesis 1:11–16). But when He created human beings, He did something different. He took some dust of the ground that He had already made and formed a man. Then He breathed His own life into that man, and “man became a living soul” (Genesis 2:7). When He created the first woman, Eve, He also used that which He had already made. He caused Adam to fall into a deep sleep and took a rib from his side and formed a woman (Genesis 2:21).
God is the Designer and Craftsman of everything in the universe, and He created human beings a step above all

[SOURCE: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Creator_deity]
CONTENT: Creator deity
| Part of a series on |
|
|---|
A creator deity or creator god is a [deity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deity) responsible for the creation of the [Earth](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Earth), [world](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World#Theology), and [universe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universe) in human religion and mythology. In [monotheism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monotheism), the single [God](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/God) is often also the creator. A number of [monolatristic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monolatrism) traditions separate a secondary creator from a primary [transcendent](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transcendence_(religion)) being, identified as a primary creator.[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org#cite_note-Hindus-1)
Monotheism
Atenism
Initiated by Pharaoh [Akhenaten](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akhenaten) and [Queen Nefertiti](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nefertiti) around 1330 BCE, during the [New Kingdom period](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_Kingdom_of_Egypt) in ancient Egyptian history. They built an entirely new capital city ([Akhetaten](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akhetaten)) for themselves and worshippers of their sole creator god in a wilderness. His father used to worship [Aten](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aten) alongside other gods of their polytheistic religion. Aten, for a long time before his father's time, was revered as a god among the many gods and goddesses in Egypt. Atenism was countermanded by later pharaoh [Tutankhamun](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tutankhamun), as chronicled in the artifact, the [Restoration Stela](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Restoration_Stela).[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org#cite_note-2) Despite different views, Atenism is considered by some scholars to be one of the frontiers of monotheism in human history.
Abrahamic religions
Judaism
The [Genesis creation narrative](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Genesis_creation_narrative) is the [creation myth](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Creation_myth)[[a]](https://en.wikipedia.org#cite_note-3) of both [Judaism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Judaism) and [Christianity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christianity).[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org#cite_note-FOOTNOTELeemingLeeming2004113-4) The [narrative](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Narrative) is made up of two stories, roughly equivalent to the first two chapters of the [Book of Genesis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Book_of_Genesis). In the first, [Elohim](https://en.

The term "evolution" usually refers to the biological evolution of living things. But the processes by which planets, stars, galaxies, and the universe form and change over time are also types of "evolution." In all of these cases there is change over time, although the processes involved are quite different.
In the late 1920s the American astronomer Edwin Hubble made a very interesting and important discovery. Hubble made observations that he interpreted as showing that distant stars and galaxies are receding from Earth in every direction. Moreover, the velocities of recession increase in proportion with distance, a discovery that has been confirmed by numerous and repeated measurements since Hubble's time. The implication of these findings is that the universe is expanding.
Hubble's hypothesis of an expanding universe leads to certain deductions. One is that the universe was more condensed at a previous time. From this deduction came the suggestion that all the currently observed matter and energy in the universe were initially condensed in a very small and infinitely hot mass. A huge explosion, known as the Big Bang, then sent matter and energy expanding in all directions.
This Big Bang hypothesis led to more testable deductions. One such deduction was that the temperature in deep space today should be several degrees above absolute zero. Observations showed this deduction to be correct. In fact, the Cosmic Microwave Background Explorer (COBE) satellite launched in 1991 confirmed that the background radiation field has exactly the spectrum predicted by a Big Bang origin for the universe.
As the universe expanded, according to current scientific understanding, matter collected into clouds that began to condense and rotate, forming the forerunners of galaxies. Within galaxies, including our own Milky Way galaxy, changes in pressure caused gas and dust to form distinct clouds. CONTENT: Table of Contents
The [Bible](https://bibleask.org/what-is-the-bible/) opens with the foundational [truth](https://bibleask.org/what-is-truth/) that [God](https://bibleask.org/who-is-god/) is the Creator of [heaven](https://bibleask.org/is-heaven-a-real-place/) and [earth](https://bibleask.org/what-is-earth-to-god/). This declaration forms the basis for understanding [God](https://bibleask.org/who-is-god/)’s character, His authority, and His relationship with humanity. Scripture presents creation not as a random event but as an intentional act of a loving and powerful Creator who brought all things into existence for His purpose and glory. From Genesis to Revelation, the Word of [God](https://bibleask.org/who-is-god/) affirms that everything in the universe originates from Him and is sustained by His will.
Recognizing God as Creator shapes how we view ourselves, the world around us, and God’s plan of redemption. The same God who formed the world from nothing is the One who restores fallen humanity through [Jesus](https://bibleask.org/who-is-jesus/) [Christ](https://bibleask.org/what-is-the-meaning-of-christ/).
God The Creator Of All Things
The prophets, apostles, and [Jesus](https://bibleask.org/who-is-jesus/) [Christ](https://bibleask.org/what-is-the-meaning-of-christ/) Himself affirmed that God created all that exists. Isaiah declared, “Have you not known? Have you not heard? The everlasting God, the Lord, the Creator of the ends of the [earth](https://bibleask.org/what-is-earth-to-god/), neither faints nor is weary” [(Isaiah 40:28)](https://bibleask.org/bible/isaiah/40/28/). Creation reveals His infinite power, [wisdom](https://bibleask.org/what-does-the-bible-say-about-wisdom/), and majesty.

[SOURCE: https://www.britannica.com/science/How-Was-Earth-Created]
CONTENT: How Was Earth Created?
[Earth](https://www.britannica.com/place/Earth)’s creation is a fascinating tale of [interstellar](https://www.britannica.com/dictionary/interstellar) material coming together to form our [planet](https://www.britannica.com/science/planet). It all began around 4.6 billion years ago when a massive cloud of [dust](https://www.britannica.com/science/dust) and [gas](https://www.britannica.com/science/gas-state-of-matter), known as the [solar nebula](https://www.britannica.com/science/solar-nebula), began to collapse under its own [gravity](https://www.britannica.com/science/gravity-physics). At the center of this collapse, the [Sun](https://www.britannica.com/place/Sun) formed, while the remaining material flattened into a spinning disk. Within this disk, particles began to stick together through the process of [accretion](https://www.britannica.com/science/accretion-disk), gradually forming the [planets](https://www.britannica.com/science/planet) of our [solar system](https://www.britannica.com/science/solar-system), including Earth.
As the particles condensed, they formed larger fragments and eventually [planetesimals](https://www.britannica.com/science/planetesimal), which are the building blocks of planets. Earth formed from a mixture of rocky and [metallic](https://www.britannica.com/science/metal-chemistry) planetesimals. As these bodies collided and merged, they grew larger and their gravitational pull increased, allowing them to attract even more material. This process continued until Earth reached its current size.
As Earth grew, it underwent a process called [differentiation](https://www.britannica.com/science/differentiation-planetary-science), heating up due to the [decay](https://www.britannica.com/dictionary/decay) of [radioactive isotopes](https://www.britannica.com/science/radioactive-isotope), [gravitational energy](https://www.britannica.com/science/gravity-physics), and impacts from other bodies. This heat caused the planet to partially melt, allowing heavier [elements](https://www.britannica.com/science/chemical-element) like iron and [nickel](https://www.britannica.com/science/nickel-chemical-element) to sink towards the center, forming Earth’s core. Meanwhile, lighter elements rose to the surface, creating the crust. This separation of materials was crucial in shaping Earth’s internal structure and setting the stage for the development of its [atmosphere](https://www.britannica.com/science/atmosphere) and [oceans](h
